codeReview.summary: Full MCP protocol implementation with tool annotations, outputSchema, and dynamic tool discovery. Implementation is correct, idiomatic Rust, and all expected behaviors are covered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the Rust skill procedures correctly. No deviations from the documented skill workflow were noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, no deviations listed in handoff

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows the project's Rust conventions as documented in AGENTS.md: async_trait for async traits, thiserror::Error pattern, tracing for logging, proper error propagation with ? operator.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-tools/src/mcp.rs uses #[async_trait], proper error handling with SwellError, tracing macros for logging

sharedStateObservations.observation: No external services were required for this feature. The feature implements pure MCP protocol logic without requiring any running services.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows no service startup commands; implementation uses in-memory process spawning via tokio::process::Command

summary: Review passed. The mcp-protocol-full feature correctly implements all required MCP protocol features: tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint), outputSchema support, dynamic tool discovery via notifications/tools/list_changed, and capability negotiation during handshake. The implementation properly integrates with the Tool trait, using annotations for risk level classification and permission tier assignment. 11 new tests cover the new functionality and all 72 MCP-related tests pass. No blocking issues found.
